name: American Bank And Trust Company, National Association
description_short: Community bank serving Iowa and Illinois with personal and business banking, wealth management, and lending services across 8 branch locations.
description_long:
American Bank & Trust Company, National Association is a community-focused bank operating in Iowa and Illinois with a primary service area in the Quad Cities region (Davenport, Iowa and Rock Island/Moline, Illinois) plus an additional location in Elburn, Illinois. The bank has been positioned as a relationship-driven alternative to one-size-fits-all banking, emphasizing personalized service and local decision-making.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal checking and savings accounts (with tiered interest rates up to 0.15% APY), business banking solutions, mortgage lending, home equity loans, personal and auto loans, credit cards, and trust/wealth management services. They provide digital banking capabilities including online account access, mobile apps for iOS and Android, and 24/7 web chat support. Their product lines span from youth accounts (Mint Checking for ages 13-24) to senior-focused offerings (55+ Checking) to premium wealth management services.

The bank distinguishes itself through local branch presence with specialized wealth management offices, financial wellness tools, relationship packaging options, and a stated commitment to personalized service. They maintain multiple branches in the Quad Cities area rather than consolidating locations, and offer dedicated commercial loan officers and mortgage loan originators. The brand promise emphasizes "unwavering service" and "innovative products."

As a traditional community bank, American Bank & Trust serves customers seeking local relationship banking with physical branch access, but their service area is geographically limited to Iowa and Illinois. Interest rates on deposit products are modest (0.15% APY maximum), and the bank requires minimum balances for some accounts ($1,000 minimum for Preferred Plus Checking to avoid fees). They appear to be a legitimate, established financial institution rather than a lender of last resort, targeting mainstream consumers and businesses seeking community banking relationships.
services: Personal checking accounts (Preferred Plus, Mint, 55+ accounts with varying rates and features)
SBA lending and small business loans
Commercial real estate and equipment lending
Trust account management and estate planning
pros: Multiple branch locations in Quad Cities area (6 branches in Rock Island/Moline/Davenport region) providing in-person service
Tiered interest rates on Preferred Plus Checking up to 0.15% APY for balances $100,000+
Free ATM access at any American Bank & Trust ATM location plus foreign ATM reimbursement (Mint Checking)
No overdraft fees on youth accounts (Mint Checking ages 13-24) and 55+ accounts
Dedicated wealth management services with specialized loan officers and financial planning
Multiple checking account options tailored to age groups (youth, general, senior) and balance levels
Mobile app availability for iOS and Android with business and personal options
cons: Limited geographic service area (Iowa and Illinois only, primarily Quad Cities region)
Modest interest rates compared to online banks (0.15% APY maximum is below national online savings rates)
$10 monthly fee on Preferred Plus Checking if average daily balance falls below $1,000
Preferred Plus Checking requires higher minimum balance ($1,000) to avoid fees while other options lack interest entirely
No indication of CDIC insurance or depositor protection details prominently featured on website
best_for: Quad Cities residents and Illinois/Iowa community members seeking local branch banking relationships
Small business owners needing personalized commercial lending and local decision-making
High-net-worth individuals interested in wealth management, trust services, and financial planning
Seniors (55+) and youth (13-24) seeking age-appropriate account products with reduced fees
diagnosis: Best for Midwest residents in Iowa/Illinois seeking traditional community banking with local branches, personalized service, and relationship-based lending. Main caveat: Limited geographic availability and modest deposit interest rates mean this is primarily a convenience and service play rather than a rate-competitive option for savers.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
